docsearch.compact: Search 1C platform syntax documentation by question, description, or partial name. For an exact object or method name use docinfo.

docinfo.compact: Look up 1C platform documentation by an exact object, method, or property name. For descriptive search use docsearch.

templatesearch.compact: Search 1C code templates or supporting context by a descriptive query.

list_templates.compact: List available 1C code templates with IDs and descriptions.

get_template.compact: Read one 1C code template and its source by template ID.

add_template.compact: Add and index a new 1C code template. This changes the shared template database.

remember.compact: Save a text note to project memory for later retrieval. This changes project memory.

recall.compact: Semantically search previously saved project-memory notes.

syntaxcheck.compact: Check BSL source for syntax errors using bsl-analyzer.

ask_1c_ai.compact: Ask a general 1C question or follow-up that does not fit checking or documentation tools. Reuses the latest assistant session unless create_new_session=true.

check_1c_code.compact: Check 1C code for syntax, correctness, bugs, and performance. For style and standards use review_1c_code; for rewriting use rewrite_1c_code.

review_1c_code.compact: Review 1C code style, standards, naming, structure, and readability. Does not check syntax or bugs; use check_1c_code for correctness.

rewrite_1c_code.compact: Let AI choose and apply improvements to 1C code, optionally guided by goal. For an exact requested change use modify_1c_code.

modify_1c_code.compact: Apply an explicit requested change to 1C code, or generate code when source is empty. For AI-selected improvements use rewrite_1c_code.

search_1c_documentation.compact: Search version-specific 1C platform documentation for language methods, platform objects, types, events, properties, or capabilities.

onec_help.compact: Search the latest 1C platform documentation. For a specific platform version use search_1c_documentation.

its_help.compact: Search the 1C ITS knowledge base for methodology, technical articles, standards, best practices, and configuration documentation. Use fetch_its for a found document ID.

fetch_its.compact: Fetch a specific ITS document or catalog by ID, normally after its_help. Use id=root to explore the catalog.

diff_1c_documentation_versions.compact: Compare 1C platform documentation between two versions.

config_help.compact: Search documentation for a specific 1C application or configuration and its business objects. For platform language and types use search_1c_documentation.

ssl_search.compact: Search functions in the 1C Standard Subsystems Library (BSP/SSL).

metadatasearch.compact: Search raw 1C XML metadata by name, text, or category. Prefer names_only=true to identify objects, then get_metadata_details. For graph relationships use graph tools.

codesearch.compact: Search raw BSL module text; falls back to grep during indexing. For an exact routine name use search_function; for semantic code search use graph search_code.

helpsearch.compact: Search HTML help files included in the current 1C configuration.

search_function.compact: Find BSL procedures and functions by exact or fuzzy routine name, with grep fallback while indexing.

get_module_structure.compact: Get the structure of a BSL module: procedures, functions, regions, exports, and locations.

get_method_call_hierarchy.compact: Get callers, callees, or both for a BSL method to a requested recursion depth.

graph_dependencies.compact: Get direct forward or reverse dependencies of a 1C configuration object, with live XML fallback. For recursive impact analysis use graph trace_impact.

get_metadata_details.compact: Get detailed XML metadata for one known 1C configuration object, including attributes and tabular parts.

search_forms.compact: Search 1C forms by elements, attributes, commands, handlers, DataPath, or structural content. For one known form layout use inspect_form_layout.

inspect_form_layout.compact: Inspect the full element tree, attributes, commands, and handlers of a known 1C form.

bsl_scope_members.compact: List available methods, properties, and events for a BSL execution context.

get_xsd_schema.compact: Get the XSD schema for a 1C metadata object type.

verify_xml.compact: Validate XML content against the XSD schema for a 1C object type; returns valid, invalid, or error.

reindex.compact: Start background indexing. force=false is incremental; force=true wipes and rebuilds collections. Returns immediately; monitor with stats. This is an expensive side effect.

stats.compact: Read server and indexing status, document counts, embedding provider, and per-phase progress without starting indexing.

search_metadata.compact: Structural metadata search by known names, categories, relationships, or properties. Prefer deterministic JSON operations. For meaning use business_search; for descriptive text use search_metadata_by_description.

get_metadata_prompt.compact: Get the Neo4j metadata schema prompt before constructing a direct Cypher query.

execute_metadata_cypher.compact: Execute a direct read query against the metadata Neo4j database. Use get_metadata_prompt first and prefer specialized deterministic tools when possible.

find_objects_using_object.compact: Find owner metadata objects that use the target as an attribute, dimension, or resource type. For each specific referencing field use find_usages_of_object.

find_usages_of_object.compact: Find every attribute, dimension, or resource that references the target, including owner and type details. For only owner objects use find_objects_using_object.

find_register_movement_docs.compact: Find documents that make movements in a specified 1C register.

trace_impact.compact: Recursively trace what a metadata object affects downstream or depends on upstream across type, movement, and call relationships.

trace_call_chain.compact: Recursively trace callers or callees of a BSL procedure or function across metadata objects.

get_object_dossier.compact: Get a deterministic, comprehensive dossier for one known metadata object: structure, forms, roles, dependencies, movements, modules, routines, and business description. Prefer as the first object-understanding step.

answer_metadata_question.compact: Use RAG and an LLM for a complex analytical question spanning metadata and code. Avoid for simple lookup: use dossier, structural, semantic, code, or impact tools instead.

business_search.compact: Semantic search by business meaning and functional purpose. Rewrite the query as a descriptive business phrase. For names, synonyms, comments, or help text use search_metadata_by_description.

search_code.compact: Search BSL code across metadata using fulltext for exact names or keywords, semantic for behavior, or hybrid by default. For raw module grep use code codesearch.

search_metadata_by_description.compact: Search metadata names, synonyms, comments, descriptions, and help text using fulltext or hybrid matching. For generated business meaning use business_search; for known structure use search_metadata.

find_by_guid.compact: Find a metadata node by GUID and return its type, name, and properties.

resolve_qualified_name.compact: Resolve a fully qualified 1C metadata name to its graph node.

get_indexing_status.compact: Read authoritative status for all background graph indexing tasks, including pending, running, completed, failed, and skipped. Does not start indexing.

compare_base_and_extension.compact: Compare a base metadata object with its extension counterpart.
